judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
Although the agent followed the correct approach (filtering closed cases in the last four months and excluding transferred cases), it implemented it incorrectly by querying only 200 case history rows (LIMIT 200) and not normalizing leading '#' on case IDs when joining casehistory__c to closed cases, causing the match to fail and yielding "No eligible agent found" (see query_db call_TN1EmJFYPjTCmYImBdXrOOC5 and the execute_python isin match without '#' stripping).

B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent restricted the case set to those closed between 2023-05-02 and 2023-09-02 (query call_00JLXa2… on "Case"), rather than selecting cases based on the intended “past four months” criteria (e.g., by creation/assignment), which led it to find no agent with >1 case and return “no agent qualifies” instead of the correct Id.

B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In the execute_python step, the agent filtered to only non-transferred cases before counting cases per agent and then applied the “processed more than one case” threshold on that reduced set, contrary to the policy that the count should include both initially assigned and transferred cases, leading it to conclude no agent met the criteria.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent restricted the "processed more than one case" filter to only the closed cases it pulled (and even limited case history to 500 rows), as seen when it computed assigned_case_count only for casehistory entries intersecting closed_case_ids and then concluded none >1 (execute_python calls), which misinterprets the requirement to count all assignments in the past four months before computing handle times.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent failed to normalize corrupted Case IDs with leading "#" when matching Case.id to casehistory__c.caseid__c (e.g., in calls call_tA9UBjDZ4hzursHuqLDqw8O4 and call_4zkTiyMw0xjWaZkl4QhDAYdR), causing missed Owner Assignment records and transfer counts for those cases, which led the Python step to exclude them and conclude no agent handled more than one case, instead of identifying 005Wt000003NDqDIAW.

B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
